page: Amazon Web Services r6id.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores utilizing an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the baseline network bandwidth is 19 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the average to strong performance tiers. The Passmark CPU score of 48180.39 and CPU encryption test of 43337.66 both rank in the top 25% tier, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Geekbench image processing also registers in the top 25% tier with a score of 15276.0. In contrast, memory bandwidth tests and Redis SET operations fall within the middle 50% average tier. LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model shows strong performance at 257.48 tokens per second, though larger models scale down to average tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6id.12xlarge combines high-capacity DDR4 memory with fast local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external block storage attachments for high-throughput workloads. This resource density makes it suitable for memory-intensive applications that require low-latency local scratch space. Appropriate workloads include relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caches, data analytics, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in CPU encryption and image processing, both scoring in the top 25% tier, while database operations and memory bandwidth fall within the average tier. The combination of high memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-density resources without external storage overhead. This instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database deployments, and data processing tasks.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.

384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8 GB per vCPU
2850 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
